summary: The SkiaSharp maintainer filed a design-tracking issue to complete the SKMatrix44 implementation by adding missing SkM44 APIs (LookAt, map, normalizePerspective, etc.) that are not covered by the current System.Numerics.Matrix4x4 wrapper.

repoLinks.description: Community implementation of Sk3dView using System.Numerics — demonstrates the gap left by missing SKM44 APIs
Related issue tracking the SKCamera.h/SKMatrix44.h deprecation in Skia m84

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The maintainer confirmed in Oct 2024 that SKMatrix44 still only wraps System.Numerics.Matrix4x4 with incomplete API coverage, and the issue remains open.

analysis.summary: SKMatrix44 in SkiaSharp v3 wraps System.Numerics.Matrix4x4 rather than directly binding to Skia's SkM44. As a result, several camera/3D view APIs (LookAt, map, normalizePerspective, etc.) that were available through the old SK3dView/SKCamera3D are missing. The maintainer wants to complete SKMatrix44 with full SkM44 API coverage.
analysis.rationale: This is a feature-gap enhancement raised by the maintainer himself. The current implementation is intentionally a shortcut: using System.Numerics.Matrix4x4 to avoid the cost of C++ interop per operation. The gap is well-defined: SKM44 APIs in Skia C++ that are not yet surfaced in the C# wrapper. Several community contributors have run into this gap when trying to replace the deprecated SK3dView.

keySignals.text: I didn't get the time I needed to fully recreate the SkM44 API in C#, so just leveraged the Numerics. Any missing APIs were just a result of me not knowing which ones to prioritize
keySignals.interpretation: Confirms this is a planned but incomplete implementation, not a design decision to omit the APIs.

keySignals.text: Current SKMatrix44 in SkiaSharp is just the basics needed to make things work. I see SkM44 does have several more APIs that I have not yet ported/wrapped/exposed
In order to replace SK3dView i need the LookAt, map, normalizePerspective etc.
keySignals.interpretation: Concrete list of missing APIs needed for real use cases.

keySignals.text: Skia deprecated SK3DView, SKCamera3D, SKPatch3D, because they provided a more flexible SKM44
keySignals.interpretation: Highlights why completing SKM44 coverage in C# is important for migration.

codeInvestigation.finding: Create* factory methods exist for translation, scale, and axis-angle rotation, but LookAt, map, normalizePerspective, and other SkM44-specific APIs are absent. Several CreateRotation* overloads are commented out.
MapScalars methods are marked internal — they are not exposed as public API, limiting usefulness for callers needing 4-component vector transforms.
Implicit cast between SKMatrix44 and System.Numerics.Matrix4x4 uses Unsafe.As — zero-copy, confirming the struct layouts are binary-identical, validating the current design choice.

workarounds: Implement LookAt using System.Numerics.Matrix4x4.CreateLookAt and then cast to SKMatrix44 via the implicit conversion.
Use System.Numerics.Matrix4x4 directly for 3D math and cast to SKMatrix44 only when passing to SKCanvas.SetMatrix.
Port camera math manually using the community Sk3dView.cs reference implementation as a starting point (https://github.com/taublast/DrawnUi.Maui/blob/2-A/src/Engine/Internals/Helpers/3D/Sk3dView.cs).
nextQuestions: Which specific SkM44 methods should be prioritized? (LookAt, Map, normalizePerspective, setRotateAbout, etc.)
Should the implementation use System.Numerics helpers or direct mathematical implementations to preserve rotation direction conventions matching Skia?
Is there a test comparison between SK3dView (v2) and SKMatrix44 (v3) to catch behavioral differences in rotation orientation?

resolution.hypothesis: The enhancement requires adding SkM44-equivalent APIs to SKMatrix44, implemented in pure C# using mathematics consistent with Skia's conventions. The implicit Unsafe.As cast already ensures zero-cost interop with System.Numerics types.

proposals.title: Implement missing SkM44 factory methods in pure C#
proposals.description: Add CreateLookAt, CreateSetRotateAbout, and normalizePerspective to SKMatrix44, implemented directly in C# math without interop, following the SkM44 C++ source as reference.

proposals.title: Use System.Numerics helpers where available
proposals.description: For LookAt specifically, Matrix4x4.CreateLookAt exists in System.Numerics and can be used as a starting point, but callers need to verify rotation-direction conventions match Skia (column-major vs row-major, LH vs RH).

resolution.recommendedProposal: Implement missing SkM44 factory methods in pure C#
resolution.recommendedReason: Direct C# math avoids interop overhead and ensures behavioral parity with Skia's SkM44 conventions.

actionability.reason: Valid enhancement filed by the maintainer. Active discussion in Oct 2024 confirms the gap is real and still open. Requires design work to identify which SkM44 APIs to port first.

actions.description: Apply enhancement, SkiaSharp area, and compatibility/performance tenet labels
Summarize current state and invite contributors to enumerate missing APIs
actions.comment:
Thanks for the continued discussion. The current `SKMatrix44` is a managed C# struct that wraps `System.Numerics.Matrix4x4` with an implicit zero-copy cast (`Unsafe.As`). Several `SkM44` APIs — including `LookAt`, `setRotateAbout`, `map`, and `normalizePerspective` — are not yet exposed.

If you need 3D camera transforms now, a workaround is to use `System.Numerics.Matrix4x4.CreateLookAt` and cast the result to `SKMatrix44` via the implicit conversion. Be aware of potential rotation-direction convention differences between Skia (column-major) and DirectX-style (row-major, left-handed) matrices.

To help prioritize: could you list the specific `SkM44` methods you need? A test case showing the expected output would also help ensure any new implementation matches Skia's behavior.
